context: Clean room architecture review — no existing code shown to O1, only objective and constraints
chosen_strategy: Option A: Multi-tier nested loop architecture with minimal overhead
rationale: A nested loop design (strategic → project → experiment) naturally manages multi-step goals while allowing each tier to remain small and focused. It aligns well with autoresearch principles (each experiment is atomic and independently evaluated). Using a dedicated low-level 'worker' for repeated tasks, a mid-level 'critic' to evaluate results, and a high-level 'strategic planner' only when stuck supports both autonomy and minimal dependence on costly external calls. Although it may seem over-engineered, careful implementation with light modules can keep lines of code under the 1,500 target. This approach remains more robust and extensible than a single loop, yet less complex than a full DAG-based task management engine.

rejected_alternatives.option: B: Flat single-loop with conditional escalation
rejected_alternatives.reason: While simpler, it becomes unwieldy as goals become more complex and multi-step. Lacks clear separation of concerns, increasing the likelihood of logic bloat.
Adds unnecessary complexity for a single-machine system and smaller-scale goals. Parallel or advanced scheduling isn't crucial here and would raise the development overhead.

assumptions: Local 70B models (quantized) are sufficient for worker-tier tasks on the Apple M4 Pro.
Minimal dependencies are possible (simple file-based memory, no large frameworks).
We can achieve basic self-improvement via storing and retrieving heuristics in a lightweight memory layer.
Rare calls to more advanced APIs (OpenAI, etc.) are allowed but only at strategic junctures.

failure_modes.impact: High if the codebase becomes too large or confusing
failure_modes.detection: Module-level code reviews showing bloated classes/functions
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep each module <200 LOC, enforce clear separation of roles

failure_modes.impact: High if local 70B model fails due to memory constraints
failure_modes.detection: System logs indicate out-of-memory errors when models load
failure_modes.mitigation: Use quantized models and load them lazily; monitor memory usage

failure_modes.impact: Medium if stored heuristics do not meaningfully guide future tasks
failure_modes.detection: No measurable efficiency or accuracy gains after repeated runs
failure_modes.mitigation: Implement a simple but clear feedback loop measuring performance metrics

smallest_validating_experiment: Build a minimal nested-loop skeleton that runs an extremely simple 'task' (e.g., compare two trivial text transformations) using a local model as the worker and logs the result to a memory file. Confirm it can automatically iterate, store the outcome, revert failures, and escalate if worker tasks repeat-fail.
kill_criteria: If total lines of core logic exceed ~1,500 and cannot be simplified
If memory usage consistently exceeds 24GB limit when performing standard tasks
If repeated attempts at self-improvement show no measurable gains for 10+ trials

ordered_tasks.name: Define Core Data Structures
ordered_tasks.description: Create minimal JSON or Python classes for tasks, results, and logs. Keep <100 LOC.
ordered_tasks.evaluation_criteria: Data structures are readable, easily serialized; well-documented

ordered_tasks.name: Implement Experiment Loop (Worker & Critic)
ordered_tasks.description: Write a rapid loop that passes a task to the worker model, runs it, and collects an evaluation from a simple critic function or model prompt. Keep it minimal.
ordered_tasks.evaluation_criteria: Loop can run tasks repeatedly, produce consistent logs, handle basic pass/fail

ordered_tasks.name: Implement Project Loop (Task Sequencer)
ordered_tasks.description: Manage a queue of tasks and dispatch them one by one to the experiment loop; record results in memory. Add success/failure logic and simple heuristics for ordering tasks.
ordered_tasks.evaluation_criteria: Can push/pull tasks from a queue; systematically mark tasks complete or retried

ordered_tasks.name: Implement Minimal Memory System
ordered_tasks.description: Use file-based storage (JSON) for storing heuristics and experiment outcomes. Provide a function for reading/writing a small knowledge base.
ordered_tasks.evaluation_criteria: Read/write overhead is minimal; heuristics update correctly; data remains consistent

ordered_tasks.name: Add Strategic Loop (Escalation Mechanism)
ordered_tasks.description: If repeated failures occur, call a higher-level model or dedicated code path to diagnose root cause and propose changes. Keep all logic in a single module <200 LOC.
ordered_tasks.evaluation_criteria: Escalation triggers after N repeated failures; logs proposed strategy updates or re-queues tasks

ordered_tasks.name: Test with a Simple Demo Goal
ordered_tasks.description: Set a trivial multi-step goal, run the entire system end to end, and confirm operation for multiple hours without human intervention.
ordered_tasks.evaluation_criteria: System runs; logs outcome; no crashes; memory usage stable; partial improvements if repeated

verification.method: Observe the system running end-to-end for ~4-6 hours on a trivial or moderately challenging goal without human intervention. Confirm successful repeated tasks, sensible logs, minimal code overhead, and correct escalation handling.
verification.success_signal: No uncaught errors, stable memory usage, incremental improvement in metric logs across tasks
verification.reversal_conditions: If too many unexpected errors or no measurable improvement after many iterations, revisit the architecture or reduce complexity
